name: Iceberg in Ocean
description: A large Antarctic tabular iceberg (~300 m long, ~10¹¹ kg) floating in the Southern Ocean. Approximately 89% is submerged. The ice is fresh water (H₂O) in solid form — same molecular formula as liquid water, quarksum tracks mass not phase. The surrounding seawater layer represents the ocean context the iceberg displaces and interacts with. Density of ice (917 kg/m³) vs seawater (1025 kg/m³) is what keeps 89% submerged (Archimedes' principle).

children.comment: Ice body (H₂O solid — fresh water molecular formula)
